select-statement.label: The SELECT Statement
select-statement.summary: How to use SELECT to read data from a table.

quiz-select-code.title: Write a SELECT statement to get all data from the 'products' table.

sketch-what-is-select.title: What is the SELECT Statement?
sketch-what-is-select.bodyMarkdown: The `SELECT` statement is the most basic and important command in SQL. It is used to retrieve data from one or more tables in a database. When you use `SELECT`, you can specify which columns you want to see, or you can select all columns.

sketch-basic-select-syntax.title: Basic SELECT Syntax
sketch-basic-select-syntax.bodyMarkdown:
The simplest form of a `SELECT` statement looks like this:

```sql
SELECT * FROM table_name;
```

- `SELECT` tells the database you want to read data.
- `*` means 'all columns'.
- `FROM` specifies the table you want to read from.

sketch-example-select.title: Example: Selecting All Data
sketch-example-select.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you have a table called `products` with columns like `id`, `name`, and `price`. To get all the data from this table, you would write:

```sql
SELECT * FROM products;
```

This will return every row and every column from the `products` table.

quiz-select-purpose.title: What is the main purpose of the SELECT statement in SQL?

options.a: To retrieve data from a table
options.b: To delete data from a table
options.c: To update data in a table
